required_sections.notes: All four required sections present with substance. Section 3 (Learning Objectives): 7 bullet objectives, well above 50-word floor. Section 4 (Introduction): 3 substantive paragraphs, well above 50 words. Section 5 (Core Concepts): 6 sub-sections (5.1–5.6) with multi-paragraph treatment, well above 100 words. Section 10 (Key Vocabulary): 8 defined terms with plain-language definitions, well above 50 words. No placeholder strings detected.

citation_density.notes: resources_source=web_search — strict mode applied. One primary resource ([1] thedecisionlab.com) and two supporting resources ([2] suebehaviouraldesign.com, [3] arxiv.org/abs/2305.10654). Primary [1] is cited throughout sections 5, 6, 7, 8, and 11 — clearly cited. All three markers [1][2][3] map to real resources listed in section 11; no fabricated citation markers detected. Estimated 80–85% of standalone factual claims in sections 5, 6, 7 carry at least one citation marker. One minor uncited clause: the concluding sentence in the ego depletion paragraph ('The research picture around ego depletion is still being refined; what is well-established is that sustained effortful processing degrades over time') has no closing citation, but the paragraph opens with [1] and the claim is consistent with that source. No hard-fail citation defects.

sequence_drift.notes: No forward references to future curriculum topics detected. Concepts from 9.1 (System 1 thinking, cognitive heuristics, automatic processing, pattern matching, Kahneman dual-process framework) are used as prior knowledge appropriately. All headline concepts for 9.2 (System 2 thinking, working memory, cognitive load, cognitive fatigue, serial processing, deliberate processing, cognitive capacity) are in this topic's concepts_introduced. One undeclared introduced term noted: 'ego depletion' — bolded and given a defining clause in section 5.5 but absent from 9.2 concepts_introduced and absent from any prior topic's concepts_introduced. It is not a forward reference to a future curriculum topic (not reserved for 9.3–9.11); it is a supporting psychology term elaborating cognitive fatigue within headline scope. Flagged as a soft metadata gap, not a hard forward-reference fail. 'Dual-process theory' is defined in section 10 Key Vocabulary; not in 9.2 concepts_introduced, but it is the umbrella framework introduced via System 1 in 9.1 — treated as carry-over from prior topic context. Downstream topics named-and-deferred correctly: '9.3 onward' and 'Judgment Framework (topics 9.7–9.9)' each appear as a single bare-name pointer sentence — compliant with mention-and-defer rule.

length_sanity.notes: Estimated word count ~3050 words (sections 1–11 combined). Task-specified range for standard depth profile is 1500–6000 words; count falls well within range. Topic manifest depth_signals note 'estimated 25-minute read' — at 200 wpm that implies ~5000 words for the reading artifact, but the corpus (including non-reading sections such as prerequisites, reflection prompts, and further reading) is appropriately sized. No length defect.

scope_discipline.notes: Topic headline 'System 2 thinking — slow, deliberate, effortful' is respected throughout. No teaching of confirmation bias, anchoring bias, or automation bias (9.3–9.5). No teaching of the Judgment Framework (9.7–9.9) — it appears once in section 8 as 'the Judgment Framework (topics 9.7–9.9) is the structured approach built on this foundation' (bare name + topic pointer, one sentence, not bolded) and once in section 9 as a reflection prompt pointer — both are compliant mention-and-defer. No teaching of acceptable error (9.10), high-stakes domains (9.11), or bias encoding into AI training data (9.6). 'Ego depletion' in section 5.5 is bolded and given a defining clause; it is a supplementary psychological concept supporting cognitive fatigue within the headline scope, not a downstream curriculum topic — acceptable within scope. The key vocabulary entry for 'Dual-process theory' in section 10 names both System 1 and System 2 in its definition — this is the umbrella framework, not a scope breach.

findings.detail: 'Ego depletion' is bolded and defined inline in section 5.5 but does not appear in section 10 Key Vocabulary, creating an inconsistency between the defined-inline treatment and the vocabulary reference section. Minor — the inline definition is present and clear.
Section 5.5 paragraph on ego depletion introduces the term alongside cognitive fatigue without a full vocabulary entry — learners who rely on section 10 as a reference may miss it. Low severity.

readability.notes: Overall register is beginner-friendly: plain language, 'you' address, concrete examples (Priya scenario in section 6), analogies (whiteboard for working memory, spotlight for System 2, relay for System 1/2 interplay). Terms are defined on first use. No single paragraph introduces 3+ new terms without definitions. Sentence length is generally well under 25 words. Hedging phrases are present but below the 3-instance threshold (one instance in section 5.5: 'The research picture around ego depletion is still being refined'). Only 2 soft findings — below the 3-finding hard-fail threshold. No hard escalation.

verdict: Corpus passes all hard-fail checks; citation density is strong (primary resource cited throughout, all markers resolve), no forward references to future curriculum topics, scope is clean with correct mention-and-defer for 9.3+ material, and length is within the standard range — two soft readability findings (ego depletion absent from Key Vocabulary, minor inconsistency) and one metadata note (ego depletion not listed in concepts_introduced) do not gate approval.
